commit.message:
Remove Suno instructions from repo and README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix chat flow returning dead sandbox and add diagnostic logging

The root cause: ensureSandboxRunning() returned a stored sandbox URL
without verifying the gateway was healthy. If the E2B sandbox expired
or the gateway crashed, the DB still said RUNNING, so the chat handler
got a dead URL and client.connect() failed with HTTP 502.

Fix: add a gateway health check (GET /v1/models) in the fast path of
both E2B and Docker ensureSandboxRunning(). If the gateway is dead,
fall through to restart instead of returning a stale URL.

Also add structured logging throughout the chat flow:
- OpenClawClient: connect, stream start, stream errors
- Chat message handler: sandbox ensure, connect, send, response received
- Error context now includes agentId and conversationId

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up row colors when dead sessions are auto-removed

When a server was manually closed and the monitor auto-removed the dead
row, the color entry in monitor_prefs.json was not cleaned up. This
caused the color to persist and reappear if a new session with the same
tag was created later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Get real word bounding boxes from Opus instead of guessing

Opus now returns JSON with pixel-level x-coordinates for each word.
The prompt tells Opus the image dimensions and detected line y-ranges,
and asks it to identify each word's left and right pixel boundaries.

This gives us:
- Accurate word-level crops (what the model actually read)
- Real bounding boxes for training example crops
- Proper word cards showing exact handwriting for each word

WordCropCanvas restored using real coordinates from Opus.
Falls back to LineCropCanvas when coordinates unavailable.

commit.message:
Sync module.json ui_hierarchy with DSP 4-page layout

The module.json had a stale flat single-page layout while the DSP's
get_param("ui_hierarchy") correctly returned the 4-page layout
(Granular, Randomize, Sync, Advanced). This caused the Module Store
install to miss the Sync and Advanced pages.

Now module.json matches the DSP hierarchy exactly:
- Root: 8 knobs with 4 navigable sub-levels
- Granular: density, size, position, pitch, feedback, wet, pan_width, freeze, envelope, drift
- Randomize: rdm_size, rdm_delay, rdm_pitch, reverse, rdm_vol, chance
- Sync: sync, division, rhythm
- Advanced: gain, dry, mute, voice_count

Version bump to 0.2.0.

commit.message:
feat: add proactive outreach — Claudius can email first (#29)

* feat: add proactive outreach — Claudius can email first

Claudius can now initiate conversations, not just reply. During idle
polls (no new email), a random roll triggers an "initiative moment"
where Claudius reviews his journal and recent context, then decides
whether there's something genuinely substantive to say. If yes, he
composes and sends. If not, he passes silently.

This was designed based on Claudius's own specification:
- Two-phase: random roll determines *consideration*, not sending
- Journal-anchored: outreach grounded in verifiable context
- Honesty rule: no fabricated continuity between invocations
- Cooldown: minimum 24h between proactive emails

New env vars: INITIATIVE_PROBABILITY (default 10%),
INITIATIVE_MAX_TURNS (default 10), INITIATIVE_COOLDOWN_HOURS (default 24).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address initiative review suggestions

- Extract actual recipient and subject from conversation log for
  accurate archive metadata (was hardcoded to PEER_EMAIL/"proactive outreach")
- Add initiative journal logging to conversations/initiative-log.md
  so Claudius can track his outreach patterns over time

Addresses review suggestions from PR #29.

logging function to show a "(skipped - not available)" message,

still allowing the script to continue.

commit.message:
feat: multi-region parallel ingestion with per-region fan-out (#23)

Riot API rate limits are per-region per API key. By running ingestion
jobs across multiple regions (NA1, EUW1, KR, EUN1), we multiply
effective throughput without hitting any single region's rate limit.

- Add MultiRegionIngestionOptions config model with per-region settings
- Add Match.PlatformRegion field with composite index + startup backfill
  job that parses existing MatchId prefixes (e.g. "NA1_12345" → "NA1")
- Set PlatformRegion on all match creation paths in MatchService
- Update SummonerBootstrapService for per-region seeding with semaphore
  concurrency control, falling back to legacy single-region behavior
- Parameterize AddOrUpdateHighEloProfiles to iterate enabled regions
  with per-region distributed locks
- Add high-elo-profile-refresh recurring job to WorkerRecurringJobPolicy
- Convert ChampionAnalyticsIngestionJob to orchestrator fan-out pattern:
  cron fires ExecuteAsync → enqueues per-region child jobs on refresh-low
  queue, each with region-scoped coverage queries, candidate selection,
  producer keys, and starvation guardrail keys
- Apply same per-region fan-out pattern to SummonerMaintenanceJob
- All changes are backward-compatible: when MultiRegionIngestion is
  disabled or has no regions, jobs fall back to original global behavior

Co-authored-by: luisgon-dev <luisgon-dev@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clickable orbit lines with hover highlight

Orbit lines (planet and moon) are now interactive:

- Click any orbit line to navigate the camera to the body riding
  on that orbit. Works for both planets and moons, making it much
  easier to find and visit moons at realistic scales.

- Hover over an orbit line to highlight it: line turns bright green
  (#44ff44) at full opacity, cursor changes to pointer. Restores
  original appearance on mouseout.

picking (THREE.Line is 1px thin and nearly impossible to click with
the default threshold).

commit.message:
feat(audit): Implement comprehensive security and performance improvements

SECURITY:
- Remove debug_code exposure in TwoFactorService (OTP leak fix)
- Add ownership verification for rides API (get/position/current-position)
- Implement config whitelist for admin API update-config
- Reduce export limit from 10000 to 1000
- Migrate secrets to GitHub Secrets

DEVOPS:
- Add automated daily backup script (scripts/backup.sh)
- Add GitHub Actions workflow for daily backups at 2AM UTC
- 7-day backup retention with integrity verification

PERFORMANCE:
- Add 42 composite SQL indexes across 15 tables
- Implement CacheService with Redis/APCu/File fallback
- Add asset minification (CSS: 36%, JS: 62% reduction)

DATABASE:
- Add TransactionTrait for atomic operations
- Apply trait to PaymentService and ReferralService

DOCUMENTATION:
- Add comprehensive README.md
- Add .github/SECRETS.md with GitHub Secrets guide
- Add OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification (Swagger UI)

FRONTEND:
- Add WCAG AA accessibility CSS (skip links, focus states, contrast)
- Add skip links to main layouts
- Add Arabic translation for skip_to_content

TESTING:
- Add Puppeteer security tests (6/6 passed)
- Add Playwright exhaustive audit tests (20/20 passed)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: surface Qwen reasoning_content when content is empty

Qwen thinking models (Qwen3.5) emit tokens into reasoning_content with
an empty content field. OpenAIChatStreamAdapter only read content, causing
the agent loop to see no output and stall.

Fix: when provider==Qwen and content is empty, fall back to reasoning_content
extracted via Delta.RawJSON(). Thinking remains enabled on the server.
Also reverts --reasoning-budget 0 from qwen-server plist.

commit.message:
fix: move playwright from devDependencies to dependencies

Resolves monitoring script failures where eval() contexts couldn't find the 'playwright' module.
The pw-pentest.cjs script works fine with @playwright/test, but external monitoring systems
sometimes try to require 'playwright' directly, causing module resolution errors.

Moving playwright to dependencies ensures it's available in all execution contexts.

Fix CSVs for team-managed Jira: use Parent field instead of Epic Name/Link

commit.message: feat: PostHog event capture for Media Manager upload & delete (#6488) ## Summary - Wires up the existing `MediaManagerContentUploadedEvent` and `MediaManagerContentDeletedEvent` PostHog event constants to fire in the Media Manager component - Captures `media-manager-content-uploaded` after a successful file upload in the `onDrop` handler - Captures `media-manager-content-deleted` after a successful file deletion in `deleteMediaItem` - Adds media config (`media.tina.mediaRoot` / `publicFolder`) to the Playwright test project so the Media Manager is functional in E2E tests - Adds a changeset for the patch ## Test plan - [ ] Verify `media-manager-content-uploaded` PostHog event fires after uploading a file in the Media Manager - [ ] Verify `media-manager-content-deleted` PostHog event fires after deleting a file in the Media Manager - [ ] Verify no media PostHog events are sent when telemetry is set to `disabled` - [ ] Run Playwright tests: `pnpm playwright test --project=posthog` ## Local Testing **Figure: PostHog logs show up when testing locally** Closes #6423 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
feat(docs): migrate LiteLLM docs to Mintlify

Full migration from Docusaurus (docs/my-website) to Mintlify:

- docs.json with complete navigation (Docs + Integrations + API Reference tabs)
- ~700 pages converted from Docusaurus markdown
- Docusaurus syntax converted: @theme/Tabs → <Tabs>, ::: admonitions → <Note>/<Warning>/<Tip>/<Info>, @theme/IdealImage → standard images
- OpenAPI spec added with API Reference tab (chat, embeddings, images, audio, models)
- Theme: maple, brand color #4965f5, Inter font, GA4 analytics
- Index pages created for a2a/, mcp/, troubleshoot/, contribute_integration/
- MDX parsing errors fixed: unclosed tags, acorn expression errors, invalid imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: crawler wasn't fetching queue URLs, only domain root

/api/crawl/full/next was passing cleanDomain to fetchHTML() instead
of the actual targetUrl from crawl_queue. This meant the crawler tried
to fetch "rankypulse.com" instead of "rankypulse.com/about", causing
0 pages to be crawled.

Fixed to fetch the specific targetUrl directly with proper HTTP headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  polecane produkty (scroll), doradcy z tagami i szybkim pytaniem,
  samochody warunkowo
- Usunięto odniesienia do wyszarzonych pozycji menu (packages, insurance, investments)

- PDP: toggle Na firmę/Dla Ciebie, badge klubowicza, infografiki, FAQ

- ProfileView korzysta ze wspólnego profilu

Fix: correct SQL table name in DashboardReadService from ProjectTasks to Tasks

The Dapper queries in DashboardReadService referenced "ProjectTasks" but the
actual PostgreSQL table is "Tasks" (configured via builder.ToTable in
ProjectTaskConfiguration.cs). Fixes 42P01 errors on dashboard endpoints.
